videos.title: MEGÉVE MONT-BLANC GRANFONDO | My comeback event at 57! | 105KM 2800M ⬆️ 🇫🇷 🚵🏽‍♂️
videos.description:
Join me as I ride the Megeve Mont Blanc Granfondo in a cycling peloton cycling through the beautiful French Alps, capturing the stunning scenic views. It's great to be back! 2 years since my last event in the Swiss Marmotte I'm on the start line at the Megeve Mont Blanc event. 

Welcome to our Cycling in the Alps and the stunning northern French Alps! This climb route takes us through some of the most stunning and challenging terrain in the northern Alps, near Chamonix, Megeve, and Combloux. 

Join me for future videos https://www.youtube.com/@CyclingInTheAlps"Sub_Confirmation=1" 

The event has 3 routes and you can decide on the day - more here https://megeve-montblanc.com/  I opted for the medium route with just over 100KMS and nearly 2800M of climbing.

If you plan to do the event in 2026 we can help with your airport transfers to Megeve in just over an hour travel time - drop me a line through https://resortrides.com/


Here's the Strava https://www.strava.com/activities/15646784232/overview I'm at the top of my age group and came 8th out of 25, and 37 out of 155 finishers.

I've recently bought a DJI Osmo 360 camera and today was its first test. Have you bought a DJI Osmo 360? What do you think of it? 



Thanks for reading and watching, and if you have got this far, please give us a like and click subscribe 

Cycling inspiration music promoted by Epidemic Sound. 

Cycling in the French Alps is brought to you from our base at the foot of Mont Blanc close to Chamonix, France. We aim to bring you some stunning scenery, tips and knowledge and share our passion for the French Alps. We are a Husband and Wife team, Mike and Jude (Judith ) in our early to mid 50’s. We upload weekly in the cycling season and all contact is filmed and edited by us. We are based in Haute-Savoie, home of the 2027 UCI 'Mega' World Championships which will see 19 UCI Gold medals up for grabs from handisport, mountain biking, track riding and the road racing events and time trials.


******* Please ******* 

Like | Comment | Share | SUBSCRIBE 

Please SUBSCRIBE To My "Cycling in the French Alps" YouTube Channel: 🙂👇👍 

SUBSCRIBE Now:https://cutt.ly/vTXuvIk

Welcome to Cycling in the French Alps. 

Timecode

00:00 Introduction
00:39 Col des Saisies 
01:00 Col de la Forclaz de Queige 
01:37 Col de la cyclostourite
02:05 heading to Beaufort
02:21 Col des Saisies from Beaufort




_________________________________________________________________ 

If you have comments regarding how we can improve our videos, kindly comment down below. 
_________________________________________________________________ 

Follow me on Social Media: - 

✔Instagram:https://www.instagram.com/breathebike/ 
✔Facebook:https://www.facebook.com/BreatheBike/

---------------------------------------------------------------- 

Other Video Links: 

👉 💢 Swiss Gravel ride 
https://youtu.be/r6hX67w3PA8
---------------------------------------------------------------- 

👉 💢 The snow walls of the Col du Petit St Bernard 
https://youtu.be/4WsgG7hCN8M

---------------------------------------------------------------- 

👉 💢 Col de Sarenne from Alpe d'Huez 
https://youtu.be/XdUNlrmZ41c 

---------------------------------------------------------------- 

👉 💢 LightCarbon Frame LCR007-V | Post Build 
https://youtu.be/QpAXSuVtq2M 

---------------------------------------------------------------- 

👉 💢 LightCarbon Frame LCR007-V
https://youtu.be/SBDR9cUd6q4 


Bikes 
Gravel Bike Canyon Inflight 
Road bikes - Fondriest Zero and Cervelo Caledonia 5

Megeve Mont Blanc Cyclosportive | Epic Cycling events in the French Alps | Riding the Col de la Colombière  Tour de France climbs | Best cycling routes in the French Alps



Don't forget to SUBSCRIBE

 #tourdefrance #cyclingalps #cycling 

Thanks for visiting my channel

#roadcycling #cyclingfrenchalps #breathebike #tourdefrance 

Join our mailer 👉👉👉 https://dashboard.mailerlite.com/forms/1206124/139171394493613120/share
videos.transcript:
[Music]
Sunday saw me back in a pelaton for the
first time in 2 years. It was a
beautiful day in the French Alps. And
this is the MAJV Mlanc event. Formerly
the time majev it used to be held in
June but for some reason they've now
moved it to the end of August with a 10
kilometer neutralized section from Majv
and over 40 kilometers an hour blast to
the bottom of the first climb where the
pelaton started to split up. This is the
low slopes of the culdeacy
and before long we're winding our way
over to the culdea forclar de I think
it's pronounced. If you rode the attack,
that's Elbertville down on the right
hand side in the distance. And under my
right elbow was Euan.
Here we're just coming up to the summit
of the Cderela forecast about 30
kilometers into the event before a fast
sweeping descent down into the valley
floor and just above Alberville.
There's three distances to choose from.
I chose the medium event on the day,
which was just over 100 kilometers and
just shy of 2,800 m of climbing. We'd
had snow down to 2,600 m the last few
days. And this turned out to be a break
in the stormy days, although it was
super chilly. Still climbing with a gel
on until the final climb of the day.
Climbing again over the culdeichlist
before a descent back down into the
valley floor again and onto the main
road up towards Bowfort.
As we come around this switch back, you
can see M Blanc in the distance
and I managed to jump on the back of
this group and get a free toe all the
way up to Bowfort.
Being my comeback event, I seem to have
a lot left in the tank when I got onto
the lower slopes of the Seaz and I had a
really strong climb, only being 40
seconds off my personal best, which was
set about 10 years ago.
If you rode the attack, this is the side
of the sey that you descended. You might
remember this junction and we've got
this stellar view of MLANC in the
distance.
This says he's about 15 kilometers from
this side. It took me an hour and 5
minutes to get to the summit and I
managed to reel in quite a few people.
Although a lot of these riders were
doing the shorter event,
this bend has to be one of the most
stunning switchbacks in the Alps as we
look towards Mlanc over the ski fields
of Lake Contamine and the Bowfort Valley
down to the right. Now, I thought I was
feeling good, but this guy is in the
longer event. I think he came second
overall. He's also been over the colder
prey.
into the final couple of kilometers to
the summit of the Seazi and it was now
in the mid20s
through Seazi ski village and over the
top of the co and onto the descent.
That's the coldest arave in the
The Majv Mlanc has always been a
wellorganized event. Although I must
confess I do prefer it as a June event
rather than August. With the June dates,
it's good preparation for events such as
the Marmott and the Atapa Tour.
I'll put a link in the description below
to the organization's website so you can
check out what date it's going to run in
2026.
The final couple of bends of the descent
of the Seazi and back out onto the main
road towards Majv.
As we approached the outskirts of Majv,
I finished in a time of 4 hours 26
minutes, coming in 37th place out of 155
finishes and eighth in the over 50s.
Quick advertisement. If the 2026 Majv
Mlanc is on your radar, we can organize
your airport transfers to MJV. It's only
an hour from Geneva and there's some
details in the description below.
Meantime, thanks for watching. We'll see
you next time on Cycling in the Outs.

videos.title: Tour du Mont Blanc, the World's Hardest One-Day Cycling Event (330km 8000m)
videos.description:
Join me on an unprecedented loop with the Mont Blanc as a backdrop, a marathon day with passes outside categories, simply 330 km and 8,000m, this is the menu that awaits the participants of the 11th edition of the Tour du Mont Blanc, on Saturday 15th of July. Start at 5:00 am in the village Les Saisies for a route which will cross France, Switzerland and Italy. Does it get much harder? I find out.

Join AlpCycles at this event next year - https://alpcycles.com/cycling-events/tour-de-mont-blanc.html

#cycling #montblanc #endurance 

Drop a LIKE and enjoy!

Thank you for watching this video. I hope that you keep up with the regular videos I post on the channel, subscribe, and share your learnings with those that need to hear it. Your comments are important to me, so please take a second and say ‘Hey’ ;)
---------------------------------------------------------------
F I N D  M E  ON...
🍌 MY STRAVA [https://www.strava.com/pros/47597]
📸 INSTAGRAM [http://instagram.com/edlaverack]
🤖 TWITTER [https://twitter.com/edlaverack]

Interested in Coaching/Mentoring? - laverackcoaching@yahoo.com

Special thanks to my sponsors for keeping me on the road -
Factor Bikes via Vires Velo - https://viresvelo.com/

🖤STYRKR Endurance Nutrition (highly rate the bars, electrolyte tabs and drink mixes if you like neutral flavours) https://styrkr.com?dt_id=313388&utm_medium=affiliate&utm_campaign=EDLAVERACK - EDLAVERACK for 25% off your order!

☕Clifton Coffee Roasters - ED15 for 15% OFF COFFEE on our website. No minimum spend required! https://cliftoncoffee.co.uk/

👔Band of Climbers (not just for people that climb!) - Affiliate link (small bonus for me) http://bit.ly/BoC-ED and use EDMODE10 for 10% off

⛰️Interested in a Haute Route Ironman Event? - https://info.hauteroute.org/promo-code-laverack

🔧Y Beic, South Wales - Repairs, Servicing, Building & Fitting - “Serving the bike AND the rider” https://www.ybeic.co.uk/

🍜Hexis - Enhance performance and elevate recovery with personalised nutrition tailored to your workouts, lifestyle and goals.  - Code EL15 - https://www.hexis.live/ - https://www.hexis.live/stories/sports-nutrition-snack-e-book

👜Jango Rolls - Bar bags and accsesories - ED10 for discount!
----------------------------------------------------------------
A little about Ed….
Ed is an elite cyclist, 2019 British Hill Climb Champion and former Under 23 British RR Champion. He has raced as a Continental level Professional Rider for one of the most successful British cycling teams in Japan, Korea, Australia, Canada, Azerbaijan to name a few. He is also a public speaker, coach, and founded the on/offline cycling community, Backpedal. At the start of 2021, the Discord community began, and plonked many of Ed's viewers in to a 550+ strong group of people that had many things in common. Not least wanting to explore the world and pedal their bikes faster!

Ed coaches lots of cyclists in the online community too! If you are interested, email [laverackcoaching@yahoo.com] for information on the 1-2-1 coaching package and training plans, of which there are many and flexible options to suit everyone!

Ed now prioritises having fun with my hard earned fitness and knowledge in the sport. Showcasing that maybe there is an alternative to racing for teams when you can race for yourself and your community of followers and brands. This YouTube channel is a docu-series highlighting what it’s like to be a cyclist in today’s competitive age of sport.
---------------------------------------------------------
DISCLAIMER🙏🏼: Links included in this description might be affiliate links. If you purchase a product or service with the links that I provide I may receive a small commission. There is no additional charge to you. Thank you for supporting my channel so I can continue to provide you with content each week.🤳🏻

videos.title: Tour du mont blanc 2019
videos.description:
https://www.strava.com/activities/2549755750

Tour du mont blanc cyclo 2019. 332 kms 8313 m de dénivelé. en passant par la suisse et l'Italie au départ du col des saisies à 5h00 le 20 juillet.
Col emprunté: Montée de Vaudagne (1106m), Col des Montets (1456m), Col de la Forclaz (1533m), Col du grand Saint Bernard (2469m), Verrogne (1572m), Col san Carlo (1951m), Col du petit saint Bernard (2192m), cormet de Roselend (1967m), Col des saisies (1633m).

videos.title: Tour du Mont Blanc - Cycling Tour
videos.description:
Cycling The Tour Du Mont Blanc
Tour du Mont Blanc by Mountain Bike

A complete round trip around Mont Blanc.
I started from Martigny, and arrived in Martigny again.

France, Italy and Switzerland – take a deep breath as you pedal and plunge into one of Europe’s most outstanding mountain vistas. The Tour du Mont Blanc is all about mountains on top of mountains.

The Tour du Mont Blanc is a classic of the road bike long distances trips. This is a tour around the famous Mont Blanc starting in  Martigny, Chamonix via Col des Montets then Col de la Forclaz, the Col du Grand St Bernard, Aosta, La Thuile, Col du Petit St Bernard, Bourg St Maurice, the Cormet Roselend, Beaufort, Col des Saisies, Megeve, St Gervais, Le Fayet and return to Martigny. On the map, it seems nice and easy but in reality you should count 330 km and 10.000 m of altitude gain.

Our Mont Blanc cycling tour through the alpine crossroads between Switzerland, Italy and France was breathtaking from start to finish.

Mont Blanc is the highest mountain in the Alps and Western Europe, rising 4,807.81 m (15,774 ft) above sea level. It is the second-most prominent mountain in Europe, after Mount Elbrus, and it is the eleventh most prominent mountain summit in the world.

It gives its name to the Mont Blanc massif which straddles parts of France, Italy and Switzerland. Mont Blanc's summit lies on the watershed line between the valleys of Ferret and Veny in Italy, and the valleys of Montjoie, and Arve in France. Ownership of the summit area has long been a subject of dispute between France and Italy.

The Mont Blanc massif is popular for outdoor activities like hiking, climbing, trail running and winter sports like skiing, and snowboarding. The most popular climbing route to the summit of Mont Blanc is the Goûter Route, which typically takes two days.

The three towns and their communes which surround Mont Blanc are Courmayeur in Aosta Valley, Italy; and Saint-Gervais-les-Bains and Chamonix in Haute-Savoie, France. The latter town was the site of the first Winter Olympics. A cable car ascends and crosses the mountain range from Courmayeur to Chamonix, through the Col du Géant. The 11.6 km Mont Blanc Tunnel, constructed between 1957 and 1965, runs beneath the mountain and is a major transalpine transport route.
Located on the watershed between the Rhône and the Po, the massif of Mont Blanc is also situated between the two different climatic regions of the northern and western Alps, and that of the southern Alps. Climatic conditions on the Mer de Glace are similar to those found on the northern side of the Swiss Alps.

The Mont Blanc massif is being put forward as a potential World Heritage Site because of its uniqueness and its cultural importance, considered the birthplace and symbol of modern mountaineering. It would require the three governments of Italy, France and Switzerland to request UNESCO for it to be listed.

The Mont Blanc bike tour: Switzerland - Italy - France
Dare to live this unique experience on the roads of the Giro and the Tour de France!

Cycling in Switzerland. Superb roads, unspoilt scenery: Switzerland is a paradise for devotees of road cycling. Level cycling paths by lakes.


A Film by Damir Međurečan
Bicycle: Giant E-Bike, Giant Fathom
Date: 24.08. - 29.08.2022.

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/damir.medjurecan
tags: Cycling The Tour Du Mont Blanc
